column1: # Parley gateway partition table — ESP32-S3 N16R8 (16MB flash)
# The gateway uses the same A/B/factory scheme as peripheral nodes (per spec).
# The difference is that the gateway's factory firmware recovers via USB
# WiFi — the Pi is physically attached and can push new firmware over the USB
# link even when the AP is down.
# The Pi can also hard-flash the gateway via esptool.py over USB if factory

column2: which is not possible for peripheral nodes. So the

column1: # factory partition can be smaller and the firmware simpler.
#   OTA data      8KB  — tracks which slot is active
#   Factory app    1MB  — minimal USB-recovery firmware; never updated OTA
#   OTA slot 1   6.75MB — inactive slot for OTA updates
